title: Dave Returns

jokes.text: Oh, God! Hey, Boo Boo Bear. What did you just call me? Boo Boo Bear. It's one of several nicknames I made up for you.
jokes.setup: Tom casually uses an overly intimate pet name after they went out once
jokes.punchline: Ann's shocked reaction and Tom's oblivious doubling down
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Tom's delusional overconfidence meets Ann's practical shock. The 'several nicknames' escalation makes it worse.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Hey there, sugar plum. How was your day?

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Sugar plum? We went out ONE time!

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Yeah, and it was magical, honeybun. I'm already picking out baby names.

jokes.text: We have Cookie Tush... Wow. Just right out the gate. Winnie the Boo, Lady Presh Presh, Annberry Sauce, Annie Get Your Boo, Tommy's Girl, Annie Banannie...
jokes.setup: Tom offers Ann a choice of nicknames he's made up
jokes.punchline: The increasingly ridiculous and possessive list, especially 'Tommy's Girl'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation where each nickname gets worse. 'Cookie Tush' and 'Tommy's Girl' show Tom's complete misread of the situation.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Okay, so I've come up with some nicknames for you. You can pick your favorite.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: There's 'Ann Perkins,' classic. 'A-Perk,' fun and zippy. 'Annie Oakley,' strong and independent.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: 'The Ann-vernator,' Terminator reference. 'Ann-tastic,' self-explanatory.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: 'Annabelle,' feminine and mysterious. 'Ann-athon,' because you're a strong woman athlete.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: 'Ann-imal,' because you're wild. 'Ann-droid,' because you're perfect and mechanical.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: 'The Annnette,' fancy. 'Ann-credible,' like Incredible, but with Ann.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: And my personal favorite... 'Tommy's Girl.'

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Absolutely not.

jokes.text: What are you doing? Is this not one of those? I might have misread the vibe.
jokes.setup: Tom apparently tries some romantic gesture
jokes.punchline: His complete misread of what kind of moment this is
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Tom's romantic delusions, though we can't see the actual gesture.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Ann, I've been thinking about us, and I realized something important.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Oh my god, Tom, what is it?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I want to take our relationship to the next level. I got us matching velvet tracksuits.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Tom, no—

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Wait, there's more. I rented us a party bus for next Saturday, hired a DJ, and I'm going to propose to you in front of everyone at the Sweetums factory.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Tom, I'm trying to tell you I'm pregnant.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I know! That's why the tracksuits are stretchy.

jokes.text: I feel like you're embarrassed by me. That is accurate.
jokes.setup: Tom vulnerably expresses feeling embarrassed
jokes.punchline: Ann's brutally honest immediate confirmation
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character truth. Ann's matter-of-fact response is funnier than a long explanation would be.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm really embarrassed about what happened.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: You should be.

jokes.text: Am I in a relationship? It's complicated.
jokes.setup: Tom asking a direct question about their relationship status
jokes.punchline: Ann giving him the same Facebook status answer she just denied him
jokes.explanation: Nice quick callback that shows Ann's grudging acceptance while maintaining her boundaries.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: So are we together or not?

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: It's complicated.

jokes.text: Been a long time, eh, Cap'n? Or Captain. 'Oh, Captain, my Captain.' From Dead Poets Society.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to be casual with the police chief
jokes.punchline: His awkward over-explanation of a basic movie reference
jokes.explanation: Classic Ben anxiety behavior - over-explaining to fill awkward silence, making it worse.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: You know, like in Die Hard, when Alan Rickman is up on the building? That's a movie reference. Die Hard. It came out in 1988. Bruce Willis is in it. There's explosions. And, um, he's trying to save his wife. Her name is Holly. Holly Gennaro Willis. Actually it's just Holly Gennaro in the movie, because she goes by her maiden name at work, which is actually pretty progressive for an action film from the late eighties.

jokes.text: I'm not afraid of cops. I have no reason to be. I never break any laws, ever. Because I'm deathly afraid of cops.
jokes.setup: Ben denying he's afraid of cops with logical reasoning
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself with the real reason
jokes.explanation: Good structure showing Ben's nervous honesty overriding his attempt at false bravado.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I'm not afraid of cops. I respect the law. I follow the rules. There's no logical reason for me to be afraid of them.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. When I was twelve, Officer Daniels told me my bike was stolen, and I cried for three hours.

jokes.text: Well, mo' money, mo' problems. That's what I always say. How about mo money, more protective Kevlar vests that save lives? (STAMMERING) Sometimes, I say that, too.
jokes.setup: Ben awkwardly quoting Biggie to a police chief
jokes.punchline: Getting immediately schooled and stammering a weak recovery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben moment - inappropriate pop culture reference followed by desperate backtracking.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: It was all good just a week ago.

dialogue.speaker: Police Chief
dialogue.line: That's not how the quote goes.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I know, I know. I was just... riffing. You know, like Biggie would have wanted.

jokes.text: May I say that the boys in blue... Don't. Stop. ...are heroes. Obviously, some more than others. Oh, boy. Here it comes. 9l11. And we're walking.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to compliment police officers
jokes.punchline: Leslie's horror as he inevitably brings up 9/11
jokes.explanation: Excellent build-up with Leslie's desperate intervention and Ben's inevitable crash into inappropriate territory.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: You know what, I think you police officers are really great. You put your lives on the line every single day, and—

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ben, no.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: —especially after 9/11—

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ben!

jokes.text: Yeah. It's called music. And there's only one way to get a vaccine. And that is to play it. To play the... You get an injection.
jokes.setup: Andy riffing on the flesh-eating virus setup
jokes.punchline: His confused metaphor completely falling apart
jokes.explanation: Pure Andy confusion - starts with music metaphor, gets lost, defaults to medical reality.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: So there's this flesh-eating virus, right? And it's like... it's like a home invader. Except the home is your body, and the invader is a virus. So you gotta kick it out. But you can't really kick a virus because it's invisible. So maybe you use a gun? A really tiny gun?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Actually, no, wait. The virus is more like a bad roommate. A bad roommate that eats your flesh. Who does that? What roommate eats flesh? That's just... that's crazy.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I lost the metaphor, didn't I? I definitely lost it.

jokes.text: It's kind of like We Are the World, except I actually think it could have a real impact on society.
jokes.setup: Andy comparing his campaign song to We Are the World
jokes.punchline: Implying We Are the World didn't have real impact
jokes.explanation: Great Andy delusion - casually dismissing one of the most famous charity songs ever written.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Our campaign song is like We Are the World, except We Are the World actually made a difference.

jokes.text: She originally put me in charge of hot dogs at this fundraising barbecue, and I dropped them all. All of them. Like, 1,000.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his previous campaign responsibility
jokes.punchline: The specific number and his matter-of-fact delivery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy competence level - the specificity of '1,000' makes his failure more absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I was in charge of the budget for the entire campaign.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: We spent $847,000.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I have no idea where any of it went.

jokes.text: DoubleTime, you said? Little brick building over on Liondale Road? Yeah. You know the one? No.
jokes.setup: Ron asking very specific details about the recording studio
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting he doesn't know it after the detailed description
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron misdirection - his detailed knowledge suggests familiarity, then casual denial.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: What are the acoustics like? Does it have isolated vocal booths? What's the mixing console setup?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I don't know, I've never been there.

jokes.text: Yes, Leslie Knope is a female person with whom I was involved. We had, romantic... Romantical involvement until I relocated to San Diego. Which is... That's in California, which is southwest of here by a number of miles.
jokes.setup: Dave trying to explain his relationship with Leslie
jokes.punchline: His overly technical, awkward phrasing and unnecessary geography lesson
jokes.explanation: Dave's cop-like precision applied to romance creates awkwardness. 'Romantical' and the geography lesson are perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: Well, Leslie and I have a relationship that is... geographically situated in Pawnee, Indiana, which is in the Midwest, and our emotional connection is predicated on a series of interactions that occurred over a temporal span of several months, during which we engaged in various activities together, including but not limited to dining, conversing, and maintaining a consistent pattern of eye contact that some might characterize as romantic in nature, though I would describe it more as a sustained mutual acknowledgment of each other's existence within the same physical space.

jokes.text: Cool. I'm going to go to the gas station and get a frozen burrito and eat it right there at that table.
jokes.setup: Leslie mentioning their fancy French restaurant dinner plans
jokes.punchline: Dave's matter-of-fact description of his sad alternative
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast and character moment. The specificity of eating 'right there at that table' makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, I'll be at Applebee's with my ex-wife arguing about whose turn it is to pay for the bloomin' onion.

jokes.text: Ooh, that sounds like an Ashley Judd movie.
jokes.setup: Ben mentioning 'double endorsement'
jokes.punchline: Leslie's random movie association
jokes.explanation: Mild Leslie non-sequitur but not particularly original or character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Double endorsement? That's like 'Double Impact' with Jean-Claude Van Damme!

jokes.text: So if you happen to see any memorabilia laying around, kindly and discreetly discard it. You got it, Duke. Don't call me that.
jokes.setup: Ron asking for discretion about Duke Silver
jokes.punchline: Tom immediately using the name Ron wants to keep secret
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with Tom being exactly the opposite of discreet that Ron needs.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Tom, I need your absolute discretion on something. Duke Silver is a very private part of my life.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Duke Silver? That's awesome! Wait till I tell everyone at the party tonight about Ron Swanson's Duke Silver thing!

jokes.text: So, we have to play it perfectly every time? No, Burly. Play it unperfectly. You mean, imperfectly? I mean perfectly.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking for clarification on Andy's musical direction
jokes.punchline: Andy's made-up word and confused correction
jokes.explanation: Andy's confusion about basic words and Jerry trying to help creates mild comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: So when you say you want to go in a more... what was the word... 'Spinergy' direction?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Yeah, Spinergy. It's like... spin meets energy.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Right, but is that a real word?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: No, I made it up. But it should be.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. But you are too hot to hide, Ann. This is on you.
jokes.setup: Tom apologizing for telling people about their date
jokes.punchline: Blaming Ann for being too attractive to keep secret
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom logic - turning his inability to keep a secret into Ann's fault via compliment.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm sorry I told everyone about our date. I just... I couldn't help myself.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Tom, you told the entire department within five minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: That's because you're too attractive to keep secret. It's not my fault you're so beautiful that I had to share the good news with everyone I know.

jokes.text: When you two spoon, who spoons who?
jokes.setup: Donna asking about Tom and Ann's relationship
jokes.punchline: Immediately jumping to intimate physical details
jokes.explanation: Classic Donna directness, though the joke is somewhat predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: So what's going on with you and Tom?

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: We're just friends.

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: Mm-hmm. And how many times a week are you sleeping together?

jokes.text: Oh, you mean the whiz palace. Yes, Leslie calls it that sometimes. I know. It's kind of cute, right? Yeah, that's real cute.
jokes.setup: Ben mentioning Leslie's bathroom nickname
jokes.punchline: Dave's obviously sarcastic 'real cute' response
jokes.explanation: Good awkward beat showing Dave's growing annoyance with Ben's Leslie-worship.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie's bathroom nickname is 'The Cistern Sister.'

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: Real cute.

jokes.text: I'm still in love with you. What did you just say? Nothing. I did... Nothing. Nothing was said.
jokes.setup: Dave blurting out his feelings while Ben is away
jokes.punchline: His immediate panicked denial when called out
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment though the setup is somewhat predictable for this type of scene.

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: I think Leslie is amazing and I'm completely in love with her.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: I said... I think Leslie is... a MENACE. To society. A total menace.

jokes.text: Whizzingham Manor. That's another funny name for the bathroom that I thought of.
jokes.setup: Ben returning from the bathroom during tense conversation
jokes.punchline: His oblivious continuation of the bathroom naming theme
jokes.explanation: Good timing and character obliviousness - Ben's proud of his wordplay while missing the tension.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Sorry, the bathroom situation in this building is a nightmare. Anyway, as I was saying about the budget crisis...

jokes.text: Was that a joke? Were you joking just now? Because if so, that was hilarious-ly awful-ly funny how bad that was.
jokes.setup: Someone singing badly
jokes.punchline: Andy's confused attempt to be encouraging while being honest
jokes.explanation: Andy's sweet confusion about how to respond to obvious badness is charming.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: That was... that was really bad. Like, genuinely one of the worst things I've ever heard. But hey, you committed to it! That takes guts. So, uh... good job?

jokes.text: I just had no idea. And so, now, I have to... ...adjust.
jokes.setup: Learning Tom and Ann are dating
jokes.punchline: Chris's visible processing and emotional adjustment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chris's emotional processing in real time.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Tom and Ann are... dating? *long pause* I need to process this information. *blinks repeatedly* That's... that's great. That's really great. *forces smile* I'm very happy for them. *jaw tightens* This is fine. Everything is fine.

jokes.text: I think I'm going to take a little go outside.
jokes.setup: Leslie needing to escape the awkward dinner conversation
jokes.punchline: Her slightly garbled attempt at casual exit
jokes.explanation: Mild verbal fumble showing Leslie's stress, but not particularly memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Well, I should probably get going. I have an early meeting tomorrow with the... parks... and the recreation... of them.

jokes.text: Mine's really just a cup of honey because tea is gross.
jokes.setup: Andy recommending hot tea with honey for voices
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's just drinking pure honey
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - gives advice then reveals he's doing something completely different and absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: You know what's great for your voice? Hot tea with honey.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I drink it all the time.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Well, it's just honey. Straight honey. But it's hot.

jokes.text: (WHOOPS) To the man!
jokes.setup: Andy trying to create rock star energy
jokes.punchline: Some kind of physical mishap followed by his recovery attempt
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment that fits Andy's character but not particularly creative.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Watch this, I'm gonna create some real rock star energy!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Whoa! Okay, that hurt way more than I thought it would.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: But check it out — I'm fine! I'm so fine! This is great!

jokes.text: I don't want to brag, but I have a ton of experience with women being mad at me.
jokes.setup: Tom claiming expertise with relationship problems
jokes.punchline: The expertise being that he makes women angry frequently
jokes.explanation: Great Tom self-awareness disguised as bragging. The irony is perfect for his character.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm an expert at understanding women and their needs.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I make them angry all the time.

jokes.text: I'd like popcorn and candy and the home team at the game. Boom.
jokes.setup: Chris singing Take Me Out to the Ball Game
jokes.punchline: His literal interpretation and confident 'Boom' ending
jokes.explanation: Chris's literal approach to the song and confidence is mildly amusing.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Take me out to the ball game, take me out to the crowd. Buy me some peanuts and Cracker Jacks, I don't care if I never get back. Because it's root, root, root for the home team, if they don't win it's a shame. For it's one, two, three strikes, you're out, at the old ball game. Boom.

jokes.text: Well, I'd like to talk to Ben. And then, I'd like the three of us to talk together. Well, then, I'll talk to Ben, and then you, and then I'll talk to you. And then you'll talk to each other, and then we'll all three talk, then.
jokes.setup: Dave and Ben both wanting to talk to Leslie separately
jokes.punchline: Leslie's increasingly convoluted negotiation of conversation order
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Leslie's attempt to diplomatically manage an impossible situation.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Okay, so Dave wants to talk to me first, but Ben needs to talk to me second, unless Dave's thing is about the budget, in which case Ben goes first, but only if it's about the Sweetums deal, and if it's about both the budget AND Sweetums, then we do a coin flip, but only if it's heads, because tails means we go in reverse alphabetical order by first name, which would put Ben before Dave anyway, so actually that doesn't matter...

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: You know what? Let's just do rock-paper-scissors. Best two out of three. Actually, best three out of five. No wait—best of seven, because seven is a lucky number, and I want to make sure we get this right.

jokes.text: Ann, I caught feelings for you. And I want you to be my girl. And I don't care who knows.
jokes.setup: Tom making a romantic declaration publicly
jokes.punchline: Using 'caught feelings' and 'be my girl' like he's in middle school
jokes.explanation: Tom's juvenile romantic language contrasts with his attempt at grand gesture.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I caught feelings for you. Will you be my girl?

jokes.text: That information is... It's not pertinent, frankly, at this juncture. I just said to you one thing. And you're contriring me. I don't think that's a word.
jokes.setup: Dave trying to sound official and dismissive
jokes.punchline: Making up the word 'contriring' and Ben correcting him
jokes.explanation: Good character moment where Dave's attempt at authority fails via made-up words.

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: That's just contriring nonsense.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Do you mean 'contriving'?

jokes.text: What are you doing? I'm doing that. Are you serious? You brought it on yourself.
jokes.setup: Dave apparently handcuffing Ben
jokes.punchline: Dave's matter-of-fact justification for police overreach
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the rivalry through abuse of police power, though somewhat predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Dave, why are you handcuffing me?

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: You were jaywalking.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I was crossing at the crosswalk!

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: Yeah, but you were crossing diagonally. That's two jaywalks.

jokes.text: He said, 'I've got somewhere important to go, and I don't respect her as a woman.' He said that. And then he acted more effeminate than he does in front of you.
jokes.setup: Dave explaining why Ben left
jokes.punchline: Obvious lies designed to make Ben look bad
jokes.explanation: Dave's transparent lies show his desperation but aren't particularly clever.

dialogue.line: Obvious lies designed to make Ben look bad

jokes.text: Haverford Playbook move number two. Ladies love a guy waiting for them in the rain.
jokes.setup: Tom getting rained on outside the studio
jokes.punchline: Thinking this is a strategic romantic move from his 'playbook'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom delusion - turning accidental misery into strategic romance.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Is this rain? This is perfect. This is straight out of my playbook. Women love a guy who's wet and confused.

jokes.text: Ann? Boo Boo?
jokes.setup: Tom trying to get Ann's attention
jokes.punchline: Reverting to the nickname from the opening that she hated
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Tom learned nothing from the episode's opening.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Ann! Ann! Hey Ann!

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: What, Tom?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I just wanted to say... you're looking really nice today, Ann Perkins.

jokes.text: What happened to you? I was waiting outside in the rain for you. Because I thought you would come out and be like, 'Oh, he's all wet. That's so romantic.' But you didn't. What on earth would make you think I would like that? Movies.
jokes.setup: Ann asking about Tom's bedraggled appearance
jokes.punchline: Tom's one-word explanation: 'Movies'
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character truth. Tom's entire romantic worldview explained in one word.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Why do you look so terrible?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Movies

jokes.text: He's shrimpy. And he's small. Shrimpy or not, he's smart and he's cute and he's kind and he's funny. He's got a great face and nice hair, and he's... Okay, that's... I get it. I surrender.
jokes.setup: Dave criticizing Ben's physical appearance
jokes.punchline: Leslie's increasingly passionate defense overwhelming Dave
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Leslie's loyalty and Dave's defeat through sheer enthusiasm.

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: Ben's not exactly a physical specimen. I mean, look at him.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Are you kidding me? Ben is incredibly attractive! He's got those piercing blue eyes, that strong jawline, and he's clearly very fit.

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: I don't know, he seems pretty—

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Pretty what? Pretty amazing? Because he is! He's smart, he's kind, he's dedicated, and yes, he is VERY handsome. In fact, I think you're crazy for not seeing it.

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: I was just—

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: And another thing! Ben has the most incredible smile and when he laughs it's like the whole room lights up and I could listen to him talk about municipal governance all day long because he's brilliant and talented and I love him, okay?!

jokes.text: A few weeks ago, a guy called me a bitch in a bowling alley, and he punched him in the face.
jokes.setup: Leslie proving Ben isn't shrimpy
jokes.punchline: Unexpected story of Ben punching someone
jokes.explanation: Great character reveal that subverts Ben's mild-mannered image while showing Leslie's pride.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ben is not shrimpy. He once punched a guy.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, we've talked about this. He was harassing a horse.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Still counts as punching someone.

jokes.text: Where is that saxophone coming from? I don't know. I don't know the first thing about music.
jokes.setup: Saxophone music suddenly appearing in their recording
jokes.punchline: Ron's denial while his saxophone playing is obviously being added
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment maintaining his secret identity while clearly helping. Great payoff to Duke Silver setup.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Ron, is that you playing the saxophone?

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Absolutely not. I don't play the saxophone.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: But we can literally hear it right now.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: That's impossible. I have no idea what you're talking about.

jokes.text: Pretty sweet sauce in there, eh, Ace? What's wrong with you? I don't know.
jokes.setup: Dave using weird slang with Ben
jokes.punchline: Ben's blunt question and Dave's honest confusion about himself
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dave's failed attempt at coolness and moment of self-awareness.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: What are you even talking about right now?

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: I'm just keeping it real, bro. You know, staying fresh, staying fly.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Do you actually know what any of those words mean?

dialogue.speaker: Dave
dialogue.line: Honestly? Not really. I just hear people say them and I repeat them.

jokes.text: (SINGING) Baby, I'm sorry for how I acted But if you give me another chance I could be the boo of your dreams, girl So when you're sleeping and you're dreaming of a boo I want that boo to be me
jokes.setup: Tom making another romantic gesture
jokes.punchline: His terrible improvised song with juvenile lyrics
jokes.explanation: Tom's awful romantic song fits character but the execution is more cringe than clever.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Baby, I wrote you a song. *clears throat and starts singing off-key* You're so fine, you blow my mind, hey Tom! You're so fine, you blow my mind, hey Tom!

jokes.text: The four sweetest words in the English language. 'You wore me down.'
jokes.setup: Ann agreeing to date Tom after his persistence
jokes.punchline: Tom treating 'you wore me down' as romantic success
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom delusion - treating exhaustion as romance. Great quotable line that captures his entire approach.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: You wore me down.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: That's the sweetest thing you've ever said to me.

jokes.text: You're not waiting. No. It feels like you're being overly respectful of all these cops, and you're letting them go in all in front of you. No, no, no. I'm just standing here.
jokes.setup: Ben standing outside the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Leslie's recognition of his cop fear making him overly deferential
jokes.explanation: Good character callback showing how Ben's cop fear manifests in polite behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ben? Why are you standing outside the bathroom?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I was just... waiting.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: For what?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: For you to be done. I didn't want to interrupt.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ben, you have a weird thing about authority figures. You're being way too deferential.

tmdb_overview: While trying to get an endorsement from the Chief of Police, Leslie and Ben awkwardly run into Leslie's old flame, Dave. Meanwhile, Andy and the rest of the office work on a theme song for Leslie's campaign.
insight: Dave's return sparks 45 jokes in 45 minutes—character comedy escalates past comfort zones.
